items.title: From PGP to Mythos: a brief history of export controls that didn’t stop anyone
items.summary: For the last 30 years, stopping the flow of cybersecurity-related software has proven to be ineffective. It's unclear why it would work now with Anthropic’s cybersecurity model Mythos.

items.title: Is the US government’s Anthropic ban accidentally helping the brand?
items.summary: Just as last week was ending, the US government forced Anthropic to pull its two newest models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, citing national security concerns after Amazon researchers allegedly found a way to by…

items.title: Billionaire Ambani wants AI in every call, app, and home
items.summary: Reliance is weaving AI into telecom services used by more than 500 million people.

items.title: The CEO of Allbirds’ new AI biz has a plan, but no team
items.summary: Call it a startup with a sole founder and a very large seed round, but what's next is less clear.

items.title: A startup claims it broke through a bottleneck that’s holding back LLMs
items.summary: Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic came out of stealth mode last month with a huge claim. It announced that it had solved a mathematical bottleneck that had been holding back large language models for almost a decade.…

items.title: The US says ASML’s top chip tool may be in China, but how?
items.summary: There's a commercial logic that cuts against the idea that ASML would risk its export license to arm a Chinese customer.

items.title: Source: Elastic agrees to buy CRV-backed Deductive AI for up to $85M
items.summary: Deductive AI, a startup that uses AI to catch and resolve bugs in software, was founded just three years ago.
